title: Computer Architecture
description: The von Neumann stored-program architecture, CPU components and the fetch-decode-execute cycle, factors affecting performance, cache and buses, and how logic gates implement arithmetic in the ALU.
objectives: Explain the stored-program (von Neumann) concept and identify the role of the ALU, CU and each core register (PC, ACC, MAR, MDR, CIR)
Trace the fetch-decode-execute cycle step by step for a simple instruction
Explain how clock speed, number of cores and cache size affect CPU performance
Explain why cache memory speeds up access using locality of reference, and describe the roles of the address, data and control buses
Show how logic gates implement binary addition in the ALU, using a half-adder built from XOR and AND gates

lessons.title: Von Neumann architecture and CPU components
Cache, buses and building an adder from logic gates
